seoTitle: Create a Bar Graph in Google Docs (Synced to Sheets)
metaDescription: Insert a bar graph into Google Docs, edit its data through the linked Google Sheets file, and refresh it instantly whenever your numbers change.
intro: Google Docs can embed charts that are actually powered by a linked Google Sheets file behind the scenes, which means your report graphics can update as your data changes. This guide covers inserting a bar graph, editing the underlying spreadsheet, and syncing updates back into your document.

steps.heading: Insert a bar graph
steps.body: Use the chart insertion controls to add a standard horizontal bar graph into your document.

steps.heading: Open the linked spreadsheet
steps.body: Use the linked file gateway to open the background Google Sheets file where your chart's raw data lives.

steps.heading: Update your data and labels
steps.body: Edit values, category labels, and series names directly in the linked spreadsheet rows.

steps.heading: Customize the visual style
steps.body: Adjust bar colors, fonts, and grid lines to match your report's look.

steps.heading: Sync changes back to your document
steps.body: Return to Google Docs and click the Update icon to pull in your newly saved spreadsheet changes instantly.

faq.question: Do I need Google Sheets open to build a bar graph in Docs?
faq.answer: You'll open it briefly to edit the data, since every chart in Docs is backed by a linked Sheets file behind the scenes.

faq.question: How do I update the chart after changing my data?
faq.answer: Click the Update icon inside Google Docs to instantly pull in your newly saved spreadsheet changes.

faq.question: Can I change the bar colors and fonts?
faq.answer: Yes, you can customize bar fills, legend configuration, and axis label styling from the spreadsheet's chart editor.

faq.question: Will editing the source spreadsheet break my chart?
faq.answer: No, as long as you keep the same column structure, changes flow through cleanly when you hit update.
